analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0–2: Black–Scholes implied volatility from mid prices (r=0, T = effective DTE/365 with DTE_eff = (8−csv_day) − intraday_progress; intraday_progress = (timestamp//100)/10000 per plot_iv_smile_round3.py, aligned with round3description.txt calendar mapping). Per timestamp: IV at strike nearest S, OTM call minus OTM put IV skew, cross-strike IV std, vega-weight share at ATM (BS vega at fitted IV), bid-ask spread at ATM. Rolling z on extract log returns (window 50) for co-movement with smile width.
used_to_decide: Frankfurt switch_mean (EMA of |theo_diff−mean|) on tape stayed below default IV_SCALPING_THR=0.7 (max ~0.54 on day 0), so traders lower IV_THR_BASE and scale it in burst/calm. ATM bid-ask ~2.3–2.6 by day. Weak corr(|z|, iv_cross_std) ~0.06–0.09 suggests z is a regime switch more than linear smile predictor; still used for burst vs calm quoting. Chose ATM-relative strike filter to drop deep wings where smile model misprices.

analysis_description: Additional IV/moneyness regime study from ROUND_3 prices: derive BS IV by strike with T from round3description day mapping (8/7/6 open) and intraday wind. Compute burst regime from extract |z(log-return)| with window 50 and summarize next-tick mid reversion proxy by moneyness bands: calm_core (|K/S-1|<=0.10), burst_ring (0.10-0.26), far_wing.
used_to_decide: Proxy means show burst windows weaker than calm (both calm_core and burst_ring near/under zero during burst), motivating less aggressive burst quoting while preserving calm smile MM. This directly informed v5: raised burst IV gate and burst THR_OPEN, with low-z calm inventory flattening fallback.

analysis_description: VEV_4000 moneyness vs extract mid on ROUND_3 days 0-2: at each timestamp compute |4000/S-1| with S=VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid. TTE mapping unchanged (round3description + plot_iv_smile intraday). Output: day-level fraction in band for ATM_REL=0.22 vs 0.24. IV remains implicit in strike selection for burst cluster width.
used_to_decide: On days 0-2, |4000/S-1| is ~0.238 mean; 0% of ticks have |K/S-1|<=0.22 while 62-86% have <=0.24. Tightening burst to 0.22 drops all VEV_4000 quotes (falsifies narrow burst-only wing pull). v7 keeps 0.24 burst band and instead caps burst order size: best PnL so far (487) vs v5 (433).

analysis_description: Greek/moneyness tier in extract burst (|z|>=2.2, rolling 50): sample every 20th timestamp on days 0-2, compute BS call vega and |delta| at global smile IV (5200_work/calibration coeffs) with T= effective DTE/365. Split strikes into core (|K/S-1|<=0.10) vs ring (<=0.24).
used_to_decide: Ring mean vega ~9 vs core ~187 on the burst sample; supports smaller lot caps in the outer part of the burst band (trader_v8) without shrinking cluster width. Backtest: v8 equals v7 total (487) — no gain from tiered caps on this run; v7 left as primary for simplicity.

analysis_description: Parameter sweep sanity: compare backtest totals for burst per-order cap 60 (trader_v9) vs 80 (v7) with identical IV-scalp logic and ATM bands. No secondary IV solve — pure execution grid on existing Frankfurt structure.
used_to_decide: Both achieve total 487 and identical day 0/1/2 breakdown in this harness; keep v7 as default (simpler / higher cap). Further burst-size tuning likely needs finer resolution or a different bottleneck than order max (e.g. threshold, not size).

analysis_description: Burst strike-level IV Greek + next-tick proxy study: days 0-2, sample every 20th timestamp with extract |z(log-return)|>=2.2, compute BS vega/|delta| at global smile IV and next-tick mid proxy by strike.
used_to_decide: VEV_4500 had the worst burst proxy among active strikes, so v10 excluded it in bursts. Backtest showed large regression (172), meaning that realized fills/profit contribution from 4500 dominates proxy signal; keep it enabled (v7/v9/v8 family).

analysis_description: Book/spread by regime on tape: VEV_4500 level-1 spread (ask1-bid1) for all rows on days 0-2, split by same burst flag as the trader (extract |z|>=2.2, window 50). Complements Greek studies — spread is a microstructure control for how aggressive to quote in spike regime.
used_to_decide: Mean spread ~15.85 in both regime buckets (n burst=851) — no obvious liquidity widening to justify a spread-only switch in v11. Tried lower BURST_OPEN_MULT 0.82 vs 0.88: backtest total unchanged at 487; keep v7 as simpler tie.

analysis_description: Family12 pivot prep: liquidity-quality by strike/day from ROUND_3 L1 books. Metrics: spread=ask1-bid1, depth=bid_vol1+ask_vol1, quality=depth/spread. Built thresholds for gating tests and compared gated vs ungated spline runs with saved run logs and submission fill summaries.
used_to_decide: Data shows high quality near ATM (e.g., 5200-5500) and poor quality in deep ITM (4000/4500). Family12 ungated spline lost -2,108.5; gated variant (hard thresholds) lost -24,649.0 by over-pruning and concentrating risk in fewer strikes. This confirms the requested variation is meaningful (not already implemented) and currently underperforms under worse fills.
